arts.ttl: Leicester vs Arsenal: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Leicester host Arsenal in the Premier League on Saturday lunchtime. Preview includes team news, predicted lineups, how to watch on TV and more.

arts.ttl: Arsenal injuries: Every player ruled out of trip to Leicester
arts.sum: All of Arsenal's injured players ahead of the Premier League clash with Leicester City.

arts.ttl: Arsenal's Myles Lewis-Skelly out to break Gael Clichy record in Leicester Premier League clash
arts.sum: Arsenal defender Myles Lewis-Skelly could break a Gunners Premier League record set by Gael Clichy in Saturday's Premier League clash with Leicester City.

arts.ttl: The Big Question: Have Liverpool opened door to Arsenal in title race?
arts.sum: Last season, Liverpool entered April top of the league and in a strong position to fight for the title before winning just two of their next seven games as their challenge quickly fell away. Excluding teams who benefited from games in hand, only two sides have come back from deficits of more than five points to win the Premier League after 24 or more games: Manchester United in 1995/96 and Man City in 2011/12.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta reveals unlikely Arsenal star's wild idea to replace injured Kai Havertz
arts.sum: Arsenal's injury woes worsened when Kai Havertz suffered a hamstring injury on the Gunners' warm-weather training camp in Dubai but William Saliba has put an offer forward to his manager

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta reveals William Saliba has offered to play as striker to ease Arsenal injury crisis
arts.sum: Defender played up front in youth football before becoming a centre-back

arts.ttl: Report: Newcastle have now indicated to Arsenal exactly how much Alexander Isak will cost this summer
Kai Havertz injury: Could Arsenal formation change help Mikel Arteta manage attacking crisis?
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta should consider a formation change in the wake of Kai Havertz's season-ending injury, expert Charles Watts tells Sports Mole.

arts.ttl: Arteta identifies BIZARRE solution to Arsenal's striker woes
arts.sum: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta could reportedly deploy Ben White or Riccardo Calafiori as an emergency striker amid the club’s current injury crisis. The Gunners failed to sign a centre forward during the January transfer window, to the disappointment of Arteta, and the club have been left with a threadbare set of attacking options after Kai Havertz pulled his hamstring while on a training camp in Dubai.
